commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 이상 탐지 (anomaly detection) tab to HVAC analysis

Flags brands across 4 dimensions using IQR-based detection:
- 요금 이상치: absolute fee above Q3 + 1.5×IQR
- 단위면적 이상치: fee/㎡ above IQR upper fence
- 단위사용량 이상치: fee/Mcal above IQR upper fence
- 기본요금 편중: base fee share > 70%

Severity tiers: 🔴 위험 (2+ flags) / 🟠 주의 (1 flag) / 🟢 정상
Shows summary metrics, flagged brand table with context columns,
and per-flag expandable breakdowns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase XXII: Modular Forms & Lattice Theory (T291-T305) — 75 tests, 305 theorems total

15 new theorems with 75 tests, all passing:

- T291: Dedekind eta Delta = eta^f = eta^24; weight K = 12
- T292: sigma_1(E) = sigma_1(240) = 744 = j-invariant constant term!
- T293: Class numbers h(-3)=h(-7)=1; 163 = v*mu+q (largest Heegner)
- T294: r_4(v) = k^2 = 144; r_2(v) = k-mu = 8 (four-square reps)
- T295: Perfect squares <= v: Phi6 = 7 values; floor(sqrt(v)) = 2q
- T296: tau(2) = -f = -24; tau(3) = E+K = 252
- T297: Non-squares in [1,v] = 34 = number of predictions!
- T298: r*s = -(k-mu) = -8; r+s = -lam = -2 (Vieta formulas)
- T299: [SL(2,Z):Gamma_0(q)] = mu = 4; |SL(2,F_3)| = f = 24
- T300: 744 = sigma_1(240) = (k-mu)*q*31 = 62*K
- T301: Mock theta orders {3,5,7} = {q,N,Phi6}; sum = g = 15
- T302: First elliptic conductor = k-1 = 11; conductor 15 = g
- T303: L(1,chi_-3) = pi/(q*sqrt(q)); phi(q) = lam = 2
- T304: AM(|r|,|s|) = q = 3; HM = (k-mu)/q = 8/3
- T305: Leech kissing 196560 = mu^2*q^3*N*Phi6*Phi3; 196560/E = 819

Key discoveries: sigma_1(240) = 744 (j-invariant!), mock theta orders
= SRG triple, Leech kissing factors through all 5 SRG parameters.

Website updated with Phase XXII table and 3 new Deep Discovery sections.
Total: 305 theorems, 1740+ tests across 21 test suites.

perf: shadow autoUpdate throttle, context loss recovery, tune air speed

- renderer.shadowMap.autoUpdate=false, update every 3rd frame (~20Hz shadows)
  eliminates shadow map re-render cost on frames with no visible change
- WebGL context lost/restored listeners: warn + reload on restore (tip 98)
- airMaxSpeed 0.8→1.5, sprintSpeed 14→16: closes gap between ground sprint
  and air feel without breaking bunny hop preservation

Fix lava: real molten magma texture, tiles behind lava, bigger explosions

   dark crust, glowing magma pools - looks like real lava
2. Lava at depth 7-9 so tiles (depth 5) fall BEHIND it and disappear
3. Tile explosions use NORMAL blend (not ADD) for visible colored fragments

Add dungeon environments, portal animations, mining camp, mercy reward, and UI polish

- Bake wave dungeon and timed dungeon environments (atmosphere, mountains, ground)
- Port all portal animations from v1 (home, dungeon entry, timed dungeon, enemy, rally)
- Add mining camp rendering for wave dungeon (miner, ore cart, progress bar)
- Add enemy portal rendering for wave dungeon (red vortex with embers)
- Hide flags in wave dungeon (matching v1 behavior)
- Fix regalia zone indexing (zone 0 now gives zone 1 stats)
- Add save error notification, splash screen animation, FPS counter
- Add mercy reward notification (+3 gems on new personal best)
- Sprite icon system for achievements, artifacts, relics, skills, backpack

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(chart): include grid position as Lap 0 in position changes chart and stats (#124)

* fix(chart): include grid position as Lap 0 in position changes chart and stats

The position changes chart was missing the starting grid position, making it
impossible to see where each driver began the race. Stats also used Lap 1
position as start_position instead of the actual qualifying grid position,
causing incorrect net_change, overtakes, and biggest_gain calculations.

- Add get_grid_position() helper to race_stats.py (reads session.results["GridPosition"])
- Update compute_driver_position_stats() to use grid position as start_position
  and prepend it to the diff array so grid→Lap1 transitions are counted
- Update _extract_driver_position_data() to prepend a Lap 0 data point for the chart
- Label x=0 tick as "Grid" when grid position data is plotted
- Graceful fallback to Lap 1 position when GridPosition is unavailable (pre-season testing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(chart): warn in JSON when GridPosition unavailable in session results

When GridPosition is missing (e.g. race data not yet fully published),
the result JSON now includes a warning explaining that start_position
and net_change reflect Lap 1 rather than the qualifying grid position,
and instructs the user to clear the FastF1 cache and retry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(chart): use namedtuple return, explicit x-ticks, and deduplicate grid position lookup

- Return DriverPlotResult namedtuple from _extract_driver_position_data
  instead of a bare tuple, giving named field access at the call site
- Eliminate redundant get_grid_position call in the driver loop —
  has_grid_position now comes from the namedtuple, not a second lookup
- Replace ax.get_xticks() with an explicit range(0, max_lap+1) built from
  session.laps["LapNumber"].max(), avoiding float-tick label bugs
- Evaluate float(gp) once in get_grid_position instead of twice
- Fix test docstring: zero GridPosition means DNQ/testing, not pit lane start
- Remove stale mock_ax.get_xticks.return_value lines from tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(CLAUD-142/143/144): Dynamic lifecycle bar, country filter multiselect, Excel export

CLAUD-142: Call Dashboard lifecycle bar now reads stages dynamically from
lifecycle_stages table (is_active=true, ordered by display_order). Removes
hardcoded LIFECYCLE_MILESTONES constant and Lifecycle interface. Backend
_compute_lifecycle() is now async and evaluates each stage based on
metric_type (ftd/deposit/position/volume) and threshold.

CLAUD-143: Country filter on Retention Grid changed from text to multiselect.
New GET /retention/countries endpoint returns distinct full country names from
actual client data. Country filter sends comma-separated names to backend
which reverse-maps to ISO codes for SQL filtering.

CLAUD-144: Export to Excel (.xlsx) replaces old CSV export. New export
accepts all key filter params (mirrors /retention/clients), respects
role-based column visibility, uses openpyxl. Frontend adds Export to Excel
button in the toolbar that downloads the file with current filters applied.

commit.message:
Fix detection and matching blocked by crawl schedule

Detection and matching were nested inside _run_platform_crawls, which only
progressed when platform crawls completed. Long-running taxonomy mapping
blocked the entire tick, starving detection/matching for hours.

- Extract detection + matching into _run_detection_and_matching(), runs as
  step (b) before contributor scans, mapping, and crawls
- Batch mark_face_embeddings_matched UPDATE into chunks of 50 to avoid
  Supabase statement timeout on large batches
- _run_platform_crawls now only handles crawl dispatch

feat: Add daily SMS notification via Cloudflare Worker

- worker/src/index.js: Cloudflare Worker with KV storage and cron trigger
  - POST /api/phone, GET /api/phone: save/load phone number
  - POST /api/sync: sync daily plan (goal, tasks, subjects, dday) to KV
  - Cron 23:00 UTC (= 08:00 KST): reads today's plan and sends SMS
  - Supports CoolSMS (recommended for Korea) and Twilio
  - API key auth via X-API-Key header
- worker/wrangler.toml: Worker config with KV binding and cron trigger
- settings.html/js: Notification settings UI (worker URL, API key, phone)
  - Connection test button
  - Validates phone format before saving
- main.js: auto-syncs plan to Worker on data changes and page load
- style.css: notification settings styles

feat: add RBAC, user profile, environment config and explore APIs (#13)

* feat(api): add challenges API endpoints and integrate with existing routes

- Introduced new endpoints for listing challenges and retrieving challenge details by ID in the Swagger documentation.
- Updated the API routes to include challenges and work sessions.
- Enhanced the response schemas to accommodate new data structures.
- Added environment variable for OpenRouter API key in development configuration.
- Refactored the ExplorePage component to fetch and display challenges dynamically.
- Improved the ProblemPage component with a new IO panel for input/output interactions.

This commit enhances the API functionality and improves the user experience in the web application.

* chore: add .env.example and ignore .env.development files

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): add PORT to env schema and use validated env in server entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): add RBAC helper functions and types to permissions module

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): load user role from member table in auth middleware

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): add authorization middleware factories and return role in GET /v1/users/me

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): add PUT /v1/users/me route for profile updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add role and activeOrganizationId to UserContext and permission hooks

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add RBAC guard components and apply to create page and navbar

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add user profile page with edit form

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update platform audit with RBAC implementation status

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): return organization name instead of raw ID in user profile

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: centralize env loading with single root .env file

Root package.json loads .env via dotenv-cli before turbo, so all apps
inherit env vars from the parent process. Standalone scripts (infra db,
api kubb) point to ../../.env instead of per-app env files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): enhance Kubb API types and integrate new work session endpoints

- Added new types and schemas for work sessions and challenges in the Kubb API.
- Updated existing API types to include new query parameters and response structures.
- Refactored the API client to accommodate changes in response format.
- Integrated new hooks for fetching work sessions and challenges in the frontend.
- Improved user context and problem context to utilize new API hooks for better data management.

This commit enhances the API's functionality and improves the overall user experience in the application.

* chore(kubb): regenerate types, hooks and schemas from updated swagger

Swagger now includes PUT /v1/users/me and full user fields (image, role,
activeOrganizationId, activeOrganizationName). Also picks up challenges
and work-sessions endpoints that were previously untracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(web): migrate UserContext and profile to React Query hooks

Replace manual fetch calls with Kubb-generated useGetV1UsersMe and
usePutV1UsersMe hooks. Add QueryClientProvider to (inside) layout.
UserContext now uses session-gated enabled flag and exposes the same
public interface so no consumers need changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: change default API port to 3344 and enable turbo envMode loose

Port 3333 was frequently occupied causing EADDRINUSE on startup.
Also add envMode: "loose" to turbo.json so dotenv-cli vars are passed
through to all tasks (Turborepo 2.x strict mode was filtering them).
Fix Next.js dev script to always use port 3000 explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(kubb): regenerate hooks after merge

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: gitignore tsbuildinfo and swagger.yaml

These are generated artifacts that shouldn't be tracked.
Removes them from the index so they stop showing in diffs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): point docker compose env_file to root .env

The centralized .env commit removed per-app env files but docker
compose still referenced apps/api/.env.development. Also switches
turbo UI from tui to stream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): add initial database migration for user interactions and challenges

- Set default values for user_prompt and model_response columns in user_interaction_on_challenge table.
- Add new columns: difficulty, tags, and created_by_user_id to the challenge table.
- Introduce interaction_type column in user_interaction_on_challenge with a default value.
- Establish foreign key constraint for created_by_user_id referencing the user table.

This migration enhances the database schema to support new features related to user interactions and challenges.

* chore(kubb): regenerate hooks after merge with origin/main

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): remove duplicate QueryProvider from inside layout

The root layout already provides QueryProvider via @/lib/query-provider.
The inside layout was adding a second one, creating two separate
QueryClients and breaking cache sharing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): use atomic upsert for challenge solutions

Replace select-then-insert/update pattern with a single
INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE using the existing unique index
on (userId, challengeId). Eliminates race condition and reduces
queries from 3 to 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): consolidate editor content loading into single useEffect

Two competing useEffects could cause server solution to be overwritten
by localStorage. Merged into one with clear priority:
server solution > localStorage > default code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(api): replace sequential queries with subquery in challenges list

The scope=mine filter was doing 3 sequential queries. Now uses a single
inline subquery with innerJoin, letting the database resolve it in one
round trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(web): add TODO for mock ClassesDatabase in explore page

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
